build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: The player assigns behavior rules to robots using a visual if/then interface. Each rule has a condition (enemy nearby, health low, ally adjacent) and an action (move, attack, turn, heal). Rules execute in order each turn. 0 if there is no programming interface, if robots cannot be given rules, or if behavior is random. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

After programming, robots are positioned on the player's half of a grid arena. Position choice matters — front-line placement exposes robots to early combat while back-line protects them. 0 if positioning is automatic, if the arena has no grid, or if position has no tactical effect. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Combat executes simultaneously: all robots evaluate their rules and act at the same time each turn. The battle plays out automatically without player intervention after deployment. 0 if execution is not simultaneous, if the player controls robots during combat, or if rules are not followed. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Robots have HP that decreases from attacks. A robot at 0 HP is destroyed with a visible effect. The round ends when one side's robots are all destroyed. 0 if robots cannot be destroyed, if HP does not decrease, or if there is no win condition. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> workshop (program/position) -> battle (auto-execute) -> result -> next challenge or menu. Both victory and defeat are reachable, and next challenges unlock without relaunch. 0 if any transition is missing, the battle cannot end, or result flow is scripted. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

At least three robot chassis types exist (heavy, light, support) with different stats, weapons, roles, instruction slots, and distinct silhouettes. 0 if only one robot type exists, types are identical, or chassis differ only by text/stat labels. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

At least four condition types and four action types are available for programming, creating meaningful combinatorial depth. Different rule combinations produce observably different behaviors. 0 if fewer than three conditions or actions exist. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

A campaign of escalating challenges has playable selectable levels, each introducing a new mechanic or enemy configuration. 0 if there is only one battle, repeated templates, menu-only levels, or difficulty changes only by stat labels. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Enemy robots have their own programmed behaviors that the player can observe and counter in subsequent attempts. Different enemies use different strategies. 0 if enemies behave randomly or if all enemies use identical rules. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The arena has terrain features such as walls, hazard tiles, and repair stations that visibly change pathing, damage, healing, or strategy across multiple authored layouts. 0 if terrain is decorative, layouts are flat grid variants, or features have no mechanical effect. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The workshop shows robot stats, assigned rules, and available conditions/actions using readable rule cards or slots. During battle, HP bars and current action indicators are visible. 0 if rules are unreadable, battle state is hidden, or rules are plain text/debug lists. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets.

The arena grid has factory-styled cells, readable occupancy, distinct player/enemy robots, visible terrain icons, and clearly different workshop/battle phases. 0 if robots cannot be told apart, the grid is invisible, or plain grids/text labels carry readability. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets.

Actions produce visible feedback: rule execution shows robots acting (moving, attacking, healing), damage shows sparks or HP reduction, destruction shows explosion effects, and simultaneous execution is visually clear. 0 if actions produce no observable change. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Overall art coherence: robots, arena, workshop, UI, menus, results, and effects share a retro-futuristic factory visual language. 0 if styles clash, read as default Godot grey, or rely on mixed placeholders/programmatic shapes.

Robot sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art mechanical characters with visible chassis differences, weapons, and damage states. 0 if robots are plain colored shapes. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The arena uses real textured factory floor tiles with industrial detail (grating, hazard stripes, oil stains). Terrain features have distinct illustrated appearances. 0 if the arena is a plain colored grid. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled industrial widgets: framed rule slots, icon condition/action selectors, status panels, menus, and result screens. 0 if UI is unstyled Godot defaults, rectangle/text UI, or programmatic chrome.

Animation and atmosphere: robots move with mechanical motion, attacks produce sparks and projectiles, destruction shows explosions, and the arena has ambient industrial effects (steam vents, blinking lights). 0 if all elements are static. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
